description: Sends an HTTP request to httpbin.org using the specified method and endpoint, optionally with parameters, auth, or body, then returns the parsed response. Supports authentication, HTTP method, and status code testing.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: Construct a {method} request to https://httpbin.org/{endpoint}. If auth is provided, include appropriate authentication headers. Send the request and capture the HTTP status code, headers, and JSON body. Return these as a structured object.

postconditions: Response received and parsed into status, headers, and body data
preconditions: endpoint is a valid httpbin path

name: execute_httpbin_request_api_doc_httpbin_basic_openapi

challenges.failure_signal: HTTP 401 or response body lacking authenticated field.
rubric: Provides valid credentials (e.g., user: test, passwd: test).
Receives HTTP 200 status and JSON containing 'authenticated': true.
challenges.task: Test that a user can successfully authenticate using basic authentication.

rubric: Sends an HTTP GET request to /get.
Verifies the response contains a 'url' field matching the full request URL.
Optionally checks headers, args, and origin are present.
Failure: missing 'url' field or mismatch with actual request URL.
challenges.task: Test that a GET request to /get returns correct request metadata.

rubric: Receives HTTP response with status code 404.
challenges.task: Test that a request to /status/404 returns a 404 status code.

constraints: The API is for testing purposes only and does not require authentication for most endpoints.
Responses are typically JSON or plain text.
URLs must be valid httpbin.org paths.
examples: GET /get returns request data including URL, headers, and args.
POST /post with form data returns that data in the response.
facts: The OpenAPI definition is unofficial and covers httpbin.org.
Tags include auth, HTTP methods, and status codes.
The API is designed for testing HTTP interactions.
procedures: For auth testing: use /basic-auth/{user}/{passwd} with credentials.
For HTTP method testing: use endpoints like /get, /post, /put, /delete, /patch.
context_pack.summary: httpbin is a simple HTTP request and response service used for testing various HTTP operations. It provides endpoints for authentication testing (basic, bearer, digest), HTTP method testing (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH), and status code generation.

ctx2skill_evidence.demo_scope: P0/P1 demo-paper implementation; not full long-horizon self-play training.

judge_results.rationale: Ctx2Skill-lite replay score meets the demo threshold.

selected_candidate.description: Sends an HTTP request to httpbin.org using the specified method and endpoint, optionally with parameters, auth, or body, then returns the parsed response. Supports authentication, HTTP method, and status code testing.
selected_candidate.name: execute_httpbin_request
postconditions: Response received and parsed into status, headers, and body data
preconditions: endpoint is a valid httpbin path
selected_candidate.prompt_template: Construct a {method} request to https://httpbin.org/{endpoint}. If auth is provided, include appropriate authentication headers. Send the request and capture the HTTP status code, headers, and JSON body. Return these as a structured object.

ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The candidate 'execute_httpbin_request' was selected because it can handle all three challenges (auth, GET request, status code) with a single, flexible skill. It scores higher (0.85) due to its broader applicability and reuse across different HTTP testing scenarios, while 'generate_status_code' is too narrow to cover the authentication and method testing challenges. The cross-time replay lite score reflects its ability to solve diverse tasks with minimal adaptation.

openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  version: '1.0-oas3'
  title: httpbin
  description: An unofficial OpenAPI definition for [httpbin.org](https://httpbin.org).

tags:
  - name: auth
    description: Operations for testing various authentication types
  - name: HTTP methods
    description: Operations for testing different HTTP methods
  - name: Status codes
    description: Return the specified HTTP status
